sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content: [{"factor": "Hereditary cancer syndromes", "type": "Genetic", "detail": "Genetic testing for inherited mutations is recommended for any patient with confirmed ampullary adenocarcinoma or positive family history of cancer. Pathogenic mutations include ATM, BRCA1, BRCA2, CDKN2A, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PALB2, PMS2, STK11, and TP53 [b].", "strength": "Category 2A recommendation for genetic testing"}, {"factor": "Familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP)", "type": "Genetic", "detail": "Ampullary adenomas can arise sporadically or in the setting of hereditary polyposis syndromes such as familial adenomatous polyposis [MS-5].", "strength": "Established association"}, {"factor": "Pathogenic germline variants (BRCA1/2, ATM, RAD50, MUTYH)", "type": "Genetic", "detail": "Pathogenic germline mutations reported in ampullary cancer include BRCA1/2, ATM, RAD50, and MUTYH [39, 42, MS-4].", "strength": "Reported in literature"}]

cards.detail: Genetic testing for inherited mutations is recommended for any patient with confirmed ampullary adenocarcinoma or positive family history of cancer. Pathogenic mutations include ATM, BRCA1, BRCA2, CDKN2A, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PALB2, PMS2, STK11, and TP53 [b].
Ampullary adenomas can arise sporadically or in the setting of hereditary polyposis syndromes such as familial adenomatous polyposis [MS-5].

cards.factor: Pathogenic germline variants (BRCA1/2, ATM, RAD50, MUTYH)
cards.detail: Pathogenic germline mutations reported in ampullary cancer include BRCA1/2, ATM, RAD50, and MUTYH [39, 42, MS-4].
